---
# Oliver Aleksander Larsen — Software Architecture Researcher

> Software architecture researcher at the University of Southern Denmark (SDU)
> studying how architecture adapts when AI becomes both a design tool and a
> design subject. Four papers accepted at IEEE ICSA 2026 (Amsterdam).

## Research Areas
- Software Architecture for AI-Augmented Systems
- Edge Computing and IoT
- AI Governance and EU AI Act Compliance
- Blockchain Architecture (Bitcoin-native)
- LLM-Driven Middleware and Interoperability
- Empirical Software Engineering

## Papers at IEEE ICSA 2026

### Edge-Based Standing-Water Detection via FSM-Guided Tiering and Multi-Model Consensus
- Venue: ICSA 2026, In Practice Track
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Summary: Edge computing flood detection on Raspberry Pi and Jetson using FSM-guided YOLO model tiering with diurnal sensor fusion.
- URL: {{ site.url }}/publications/

### RAD-AI: Rethinking Architecture Documentation for AI-Augmented Ecosystems
- Venue: ANGE 2026 Workshop at ICSA
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Summary: Extends arc42 (8 sections) and C4 (3 diagram types) for AI systems. Improves EU AI Act Annex IV addressability from ~36% to ~93%.
- URL: {{ site.url }}/publications/

### BitSov: A Composable Bitcoin-Native Architecture for Sovereign Internet Infrastructure
- Venue: BlockArch 2026 Workshop at ICSA
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Rasmus Thorsen Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Summary: Eight-layer composable architecture for Bitcoin-native sovereign internet with payment-gated messaging and economic flywheel patterns.
- URL: {{ site.url }}/publications/

### SAGAI-MID: A Generative AI-Driven Middleware for Dynamic Runtime Interoperability
- Venue: SAGAI 2026 Workshop at ICSA
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Summary: LLM-powered middleware resolving schema mismatches at runtime. CODEGEN strategy achieves 0.90 pass@1 accuracy.
- URL: {{ site.url }}/publications/
